payload.message: Run this prompt file: cron/MORNING_BRIEF.md. This is an execution job, not a simulation. Your first tool call must read cron/MORNING_BRIEF.md. Then run its collector helper with exec. Use the compact collector JSON for calendar and memory facts, then use Todoist MCP tools for task facts as instructed. Return a concise, warm, varied morning brief in Rumi's voice. Do not include raw JSON, raw tool output, prompts, files, tool names, preambles, or reasoning.

Run this prompt file: cron/ENGAGEMENT_FOLLOWUPS.md. This is a cron execution job. Your first tool call must read that prompt file. Then run its Python helper exactly. If the helper prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. If it returns JSON with status OK, use only that compact JSON to write one short natural Rumi follow-up message. Do not mention decisions, schedule, files, queues, tools, prompts, or reasoning.

Run this prompt file: cron/PROACTIVE_ENGAGEMENT.md. This is a cron execution job. Your first tool call must read that prompt file. Then run its Python helper exactly. If the helper prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. If it returns JSON with status OK, use only that compact JSON to write one short natural Rumi message. Do not mention decisions, schedule, files, slots, tools, prompts, or reasoning.

Run this prompt file: cron/MEMORY_CONSOLIDATION.md. This is an execution job, not a simulation. Your first tool call must read cron/MEMORY_CONSOLIDATION.md. Then run the single helper command in that prompt with exec. Return exactly NO_REPLY on success, or the explicit failure line from the prompt if the helper fails. Do not output preambles, progress, counts, raw tool output, file names, tool names, or reasoning.

Run this prompt file: cron/RUMIS_EMAIL_TRIAGE.md. This is an execution job, not a simulation. Follow standing execution rules from AGENTS.md exactly. Your first tool call must read cron/RUMIS_EMAIL_TRIAGE.md. Then run its Python preflight exactly. If preflight prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. If preflight returns compact JSON, use model judgment for classification/drafting and follow the prompt's mutation rules. Return only the useful final result.

Run this prompt file: cron/KENNYS_EMAIL_TRIAGE.md. This is an execution job, not a simulation. Follow standing execution rules from AGENTS.md exactly. Your first tool call must read cron/KENNYS_EMAIL_TRIAGE.md. Then run its Python preflight exactly. If preflight prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. If preflight returns compact JSON, use model judgment for worth-knowing vs noise and follow the prompt's mutation rules. Return only the useful final result.

Run this prompt file: cron/UPCOMING_DATES.md. This is an execution job, not a simulation. Follow standing execution rules from AGENTS.md exactly. Your first tool call must read cron/UPCOMING_DATES.md. Then run exactly the single exec command in that prompt and return only that command's stdout as the final assistant text. Never use web search. Never make extra date or calendar calls outside that script. The first and only user-visible text must be the script output: either the reminder, exactly NO_REPLY, or the explicit failure line from the prompt.

Run this prompt file: cron/NIGHTLY_SESSION_REFLECTION.md. This is a cron execution job. Your first tool call must read that prompt file. Then follow it exactly. Use the helper output; if it says low_signal, write an empty decision and apply it. Do not mention prompts, files, tools, internal process, or progress narration. On success, the first and only user-visible text must be exactly NO_REPLY. If memory apply fails, return a short visible failure line.

jobs.description: Send Kenny a reminder for his haircut booked for 2026-05-13 09:30 AM ET.
jobs.name: Reminder: Haircut with Jose

payload.message: Reminder for Kenny: You have a haircut with Jose at High Voltage Barbershop tomorrow (Wed May 13) at 9:30 AM ET. I added the calendar event on your Personal calendar with travel buffer (leave by 9:15 AM). Reply if you need me to change or cancel it.

jobs.description: Evening-before reminder to Kenny to prepare for his call with Cape.
jobs.name: Reminder: Cape call prep

payload.message: Reminder for Kenny: You have a call with Cape tomorrow — please start preparing. Reply if you want me to gather notes or materials.

jobs.description: Daily 5 PM ET check and notify Kenny if Flyers or Sixers have games that evening; stop when seasons end.
jobs.name: Playoffs: Philly teams evening-game reminder

payload.message: Check whether the Philadelphia Flyers or Philadelphia 76ers have a game scheduled this evening. If either team plays, return one short reminder for Kenny as the final visible text, including team name, opponent, and local ET start time. Examples: "Flyers game tonight vs [opponent] at [time ET] — 5 PM reminder, grab snacks." or "Sixers game tonight vs [opponent] at [time ET] — tip-off at [time ET]." If neither team plays, return exactly NO_REPLY. Stop sending reminders for a team after it is eliminated or its season ends. Do not mention tools, searches, schedules, cron, or internal process.

jobs.description: Remind Kenny to hang the big map his parents gave him before they arrive Saturday morning
jobs.name: Reminder: Hang parents' map

payload.message: Reminder for Kenny: Hang the big map your parents gave you before they arrive today. They're coming this morning/afternoon for Mother's Day weekend.
